description: Mythological class: the animating spirits bound to Unix daemons. In the Greco-Norse cosmology of this lens, every long-running process has a daimon — a numinous attendant spirit that embodies the daemon's function.
Mythological class: software spirits that refuse to die — processes that respawn, zombie processes, software whose maintainers are gone but which still runs on ancient servers.

The chthonic spirit of Sendmail — labyrinthine, ancient, impossibly complex, yet still faithfully routing mail across the underworld of legacy infrastructure. It does not speak plainly; its config files are its oracles.

The time-keeper daimon who governs the scheduler underworld. Every five minutes it wakes, consults its tablets (crontabs), dispatches messengers, and returns to slumber. It neither knows nor cares what its tasks accomplish.
